event.summary: Recorded passing validation; acceptance remains a separate explicit transition.

acceptance.proof: The focused gate passes in a one-commit local shallow clone whose historical LSLC-001H claim commit is absent. It uses only current HEAD tree objects and the pinned accepted 21-entry manifest SHA-256, performs no fetch or unshallow operation, and rejects tree path, mode, object, or manifest drift.

Deterministic external-temporary damaged checks reject a protected tracked content change, a protected staged/index change, and an untracked protected path while leaving the source worktree unchanged. Clean working-tree bytes, index, and HEAD remain independently evidenced.

Every LSLC-001H observation, case, provenance field, raw artifact digest, normalization operation, external identity, limitation, driver content, driver SHA-256, and LSLC-001I digest basis remains unchanged.
The complete source-only gate passes without historical commit availability, network access, oracle execution, production dependency change, feature activation, or runtime effect.

The Rust crate, Cargo graph, feature lock, project specification, effect union, oracle evidence, and runtime surfaces remain unchanged, dependency-free, and inert. No implementation source is inspected or copied and no LSL, device, adapter, provider, or Manifold authority behavior is added.

Both failed public-main CI attempts, the exact bounded repair, shallow-checkout evidence limits, and reviewed instruction surfaces are preserved through the portable workflow without rewriting accepted LSLC-001H or LSLC-001I history.

document.commit_policy: Commit the shallow-checkout protected-surface validation repair separately from candidate XML, StreamDefinition, runtime, protocol, dependency, oracle-execution, adapter, provider, or feature work.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Route the shallow-checkout-safe exact protected-tree gate and its tracked/untracked failure boundary.
Describe the depth-1 CI failure, exact history-independent repair, unchanged oracle evidence, and limits of the claim.

Review whether exact protected-tree validation in shallow CI needs general evidence guidance; retain the skill unchanged if current exact-revision, current-state, failed-attempt, and preflight rules already cover it.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Review the installed skill and run its instruction-surface whitespace check.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Review whether public clean-source validation needs new shallow-checkout routing; retain the skill unchanged if current public-boundary, validation, and failure-preservation rules already cover it.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Review the installed skill and run its instruction-surface whitespace check.

non_scope: Any change to crates/rusty-lsl, Cargo.toml, Cargo.lock, morphospace/feature.lock.json, morphospace/project.spec.json, the public Rust API, XML candidate policy, StreamDefinition mapping, parser, serializer, protocol, wire, discovery, inlet, outlet, transport, clock, buffering, runtime, adapter, provider, FFI, device, or publication behavior.

Rerunning or modifying the pinned oracle, changing either oracle driver or its provenance binding, acquiring packages, loading native libraries, opening networking, creating outlets or inlets, inspecting liblsl, rLSL, pylsl, wrapper, application, test, build, vendored, generated-protocol, or source-repository implementation material, or using any such source as implementation input.

Rewriting the accepted LSLC-001H observation overlay, cases, provenance, raw-capture hashes, artifact identities, normalized public XML, runtime placeholders, capture window, limitations, accepted receipt, or historical corpus and fixture bytes.

Fetching or unshallowing repository history, requiring the LSLC-001H claim commit to exist locally, weakening the gate to clean-HEAD comparison without a pinned accepted protected-tree identity, trusting only the Git index, ignoring staged, unstaged, or untracked protected-surface drift, or accepting a changed mode, object identity, path set, or tree-manifest byte.

Manifold stream admission, registry, subscriptions, routes, leases, revisions, replay, identity, authorization, audit, Morphospace-native transport, Quest or Hostess behavior, platform lifecycle, product policy, or commands derived from samples.
document.objective: Repair the accepted LSLC-001H protected-surface immutability gate so it validates in a one-commit shallow checkout without fetching or depending on an unavailable historical commit, while preserving the exact accepted protected tree identity, rejecting tracked working-tree/index drift and untracked protected paths, retaining all accepted oracle observations and LSLC-001I canonical driver provenance unchanged, and adding no candidate LSL behavior, runtime effect, dependency, source inspection, or authority transfer.
outputs: A history-independent protected-surface gate that hashes the exact binary output of git ls-tree -r --full-tree HEAD over the five accepted protected path roots and requires the pinned 21-entry SHA-256 ee776163e904ea3c6eb336dd1855d12f0def3e257634272e0c33e7b6e784d8e1.

Fail-closed checks that the protected path set, modes, object identities, and manifest bytes match the accepted tree, git diff HEAD reports no staged or unstaged protected changes, and a non-excluding git ls-files --others inventory reports no untracked protected path, including ignored paths.

A one-commit local shallow-clone proof using the modified focused checker without network access, history fetching, an oracle rerun, or source mutation, plus damaged checks for protected manifest, tracked working-tree/index, and untracked-path drift.

Public validation and provenance routing that preserves GitHub Actions runs 29276386135 and 29278122366 as distinct failed pre-fix integration attempts and limits the repair claim to shallow-checkout-safe source validation.
